title: Life Feels Heavy — March 2025
html:
TL;DR

  • Yahir naming the heaviness: money tight, dad recovering but not changing his lifestyle, mom still working at Olive Garden and "birthing her dad."
  • Frames it as the "treadmill" feeling — going to gym, school, protein hustle, but no break-through yet.
  • Recognizes this is the texture of life that gets people to "stop living" — and refuses to.
  • Self-correction mid-note: needs more gratitude, more perspective, "more things are good than bad."
  • Closes in manifesto cadence: "don't let the moment escape you… you walk with God's eyes, internal fire that burns stronger for everybody."

Notes

A small, dense journal-mode note from mid-March 2025, written days after the Peacock incident — though the note doesn't mention it. The weight Yahir is processing here is family + financial + the dawning realization that improvement isn't unconditional ("my dad is getting better but I know he's gonna relax just because nothing has changed").

The note is one of his clearer formulations of the family cycle dread: even when things improve, the system underneath stays the same. Olive Garden becomes the symbol — pulling protein bars and gym sessions won't get his mom out of that job, the lever just isn't there yet.

The pivot at the end — "I need to be more grateful… don't let the moment escape you" — is the move he keeps making across the manifesto style notes. Sit in the heaviness, name it, then convert it into gratitude and forward motion. Connects to the longer arc in fear of not doing and max effort commit.

Connections

  • yahir family — mom, dad, brother all centered.
  • olive garden — symbol of the un-cracked family-economic situation.
  • — note follows the form Yahir keeps returning to.
  • gratitude list / dad wisdom — emotional vocabulary used.
  • brotein — "push protein" referenced as the active hustle.
